offer_name: The product development system for teams and agents
category: Product development system for software teams

promise.claim: Linear positions itself as a product development system for teams and agents, purpose-built for planning and building products in the AI era.
Linear presents its product as covering intake, planning, building, code review, and progress monitoring workflows.

cta.claim: The Linear homepage includes CTAs to get started, contact sales, open the app, and download.
The pricing page includes get-started CTAs for Free, Basic, and Business plans and a contact-sales CTA for Enterprise.

first_value_path.claim: Linear's public docs present importing issues, creating projects, using cycles, and connecting integrations as paths for teams to begin managing product work.
Customer Requests can link feedback to issues or projects so teams can see customer demand inside product work.

activation_friction.claim: Customer Requests require admins to enable the feature in workspace settings before users can create and view customer request data.
Cycles require team-level configuration, including cycle duration, cooldown, start day, upcoming cycles, and optional automations.

value_props.label: Purpose-built product operations
value_props.value: Linear says it is shaped by the practices and principles of world-class product teams.

value_props.label: AI-agent workflow support
value_props.value: Linear describes workflows shared by humans and agents, including drafting PRDs and pushing PRs.

value_props.label: Customer feedback inside product work
value_props.value: Linear Customer Requests link feedback to issues or projects and expose customer attributes for prioritization.

value_props.label: Cross-team planning visibility
value_props.value: Linear Initiatives group projects by company objective so organizations can track progress across teams.

proof_assets.claim: Linear's customer page says OpenAI chose Linear and scaled to 3,000 users.
Linear's Scale customer story says one Scale team improved bug resolution time by 52% after using Linear.

Linear's customer index lists public customers including OpenAI, Coinbase, Cursor, Ramp, Automattic, Vercel, Brex, Scale, Mercury, Clay, Cash App, Perplexity, Substack, Sierra, Watershed, Lovable, Remote, and others.

caveats: Free plan includes unlimited members, 2 teams, 250 issues, Slack and GitHub access, and Linear Agent beta.
Basic includes all Free features plus 5 teams, unlimited issues, unlimited file uploads, and admin roles.

Business includes all Basic features plus unlimited teams, private teams and guests, Triage Intelligence, Linear Agent automations beta, Linear Insights, Linear Asks, and Zendesk and Intercom integrations.

Enterprise includes invoice and PO billing, SAML and SCIM, granular admin controls, enterprise-grade security, advanced org modeling, migration and onboarding support, priority support, and account management.

packaging_notes.claim: Linear packages Free, Basic, Business, and Enterprise plans, with paid self-serve plans priced per user per month and Enterprise listed as custom.
Linear bills paid plans by the number of unsuspended users within a workspace.
Enterprise-only packaging includes SAML, SCIM, advanced admin controls, migration and onboarding support, priority support, and account management.

public_objections.objection: G2 review summaries and user comments mention limited customization as a recurring concern for larger teams or complex workflows.
G2 reviews mention missing features such as limited reporting, automation, or customization needs.
A G2 reviewer said Linear can feel engineering-first and that designers, operations, or executives may struggle to find simple high-level views.
Product Hunt's review summary reports complaints around limited customization, weak support for complex multi-team planning, and confusion around cycles, views, and hierarchy.
